questions.question: An advantage of bus topology over star topology is that

options.A: it is stronger than star topology.
options.B: it is more expensive than star topology.
options.C: its component are easily removed compared to star topology.
options.D: it requires less cable length than star topology

questions.explanation: Bus topology uses less cable compared to star topology because all devices share the same communication line.

questions.question: The transmission method that allows data transmission to multiple computers on a network without addressing all the computers is called
questions.explanation: Multicast allows data transmission to multiple specific computers without broadcasting to all.

questions.question: The main distinguishing feature of fifth generation computers is
questions.explanation: Fifth-generation computers are characterized by artificial intelligence capabilities.

questions.question: Machine language instruction codes are expressed in
questions.explanation: Machine language is represented in binary code.

questions.question: A wait state of zero in hardware means the

options.A: memory is faster than the processor in terms of speed.
options.B: processor speed is higher than that of the memory.
options.C: processor speed is lower than that of the memory.
options.D: processor and the memory are equally matched in speed.

questions.explanation: A wait state of zero indicates no delay, meaning memory is faster than the processor.

questions.question: The following are effects of file insecurities except
questions.explanation: Data use is not an insecurity effect; the others are security risks.

questions.question: The commands used for adding comments in QBASIC programming is
questions.explanation: REM is used for comments in QBASIC.

questions.question: Which of the following IP addresses can be used to access a locally running website on a user's computer?
questions.explanation: 127.0.0.1 is the localhost IP address.

questions.question: The BCD equivalent of 135 is
A good algorithm must be
questions.explanation: A good algorithm is concise, clear, and finite.

questions.question: The central processing unit consists of

options.A: control unit, primary storage and secondary storage.
options.C: control unit, arithmetic logic unit and primary storage.
options.D: control unit, processing and primary storage.

questions.explanation: CPU includes control unit, ALU, and primary storage.

questions.question: The technology used for first generation computers was
questions.explanation: First-generation computers used vacuum tubes.

questions.question: The LET statement in QBASIC

options.A: allows users to assign a value to a variable.
options.B: allows the users to enter data into the computer during program execution.
options.C: checks the errors within the code.
options.D: allows the users to enter data into the computer from

questions.explanation: LET is used to assign values to variables.

questions.question: The first attempt at using the punch card principle to do mathematical calculations led to the invention of
questions.explanation: Babbage's Analytical Engine was inspired by punch card calculations.

questions.question: The radix of hexadecimal number system is
questions.explanation: Hexadecimal is base 16.

questions.question: A file organization in which the records are stored in the order in which they occur is called
questions.explanation: Sequential files store records in order.

questions.question: The process of converting data into information is known as data
questions.explanation: Data processing converts raw data into useful information.

questions.question: A BIOS configuration program that allows a Windows user to configure attributes such as password, time and date is called
questions.explanation: CMOS setup allows BIOS configuration.

questions.question: The software responsible for allocating and managing system resources is called
questions.explanation: The OS manages system resources.

questions.question: Which of the following commands can be used to switch between open window?
questions.explanation: Alt + Tab switches between open windows.

questions.question: During the booting process in Windows, the Master Boot Record is loaded by the
questions.explanation: BIOS loads the MBR during boot.

questions.question: The set of instructions used to solve specific user tasks is called
questions.explanation: Application software performs specific user tasks.

questions.question: Personalized form of letters which can be sent to many people can be achieved using
questions.explanation: Mail merge personalizes bulk letters.

questions.question: An input device that converts an image into a text is known as
questions.explanation: Optical character readers convert images to text.

questions.question: Which of the following QBASIC arithmetic operations is the last in order of precedence?
questions.explanation: Exponentiation has the highest precedence.

questions.question: The command used to seek for assistance in Microsoft disk operating system is
questions.explanation: /? displays help in DOS.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of information?

options.B: Average score of students in a test

questions.explanation: Average scores are processed data, i.e., information.

questions.question: The usage of the keys CTRL + W in Ms Word
questions.explanation: CTRL + W closes the current window.

questions.question: The speed of data transfer can be affected by the following factors except
questions.explanation: Broadband status doesn't affect transfer speed directly.

questions.question: Which of the following data is alpha-numeric?
questions.explanation: SS3 contains alphabetic characters and numbers.

questions.question: The unit of the computer system that directs and supervises other units is called the
questions.explanation: Control unit supervises other parts of the CPU.

questions.question: An opening on the motherboard of a system unit where expansion cards can be inserted is known as
questions.explanation: Expansion slot is where cards are inserted.

questions.question: Headers and footers are used in Microsoft Word documents to

options.A: enhance the overall appearance of the document.
options.B: allow text to appear at the top and bottom of every page.
options.D: allow text to appear at the top of the first page.

questions.explanation: Headers and footers appear on every page.

questions.question: If today's date is 02/02/2008, what will be the result of typing "=today() - 1" in a cell of a spreadsheet?
questions.explanation: Subtracting one day gives 01/02/2008.

questions.question: The act of translating data into a secret code is known as
questions.explanation: Encryption encodes data for security.

questions.question: The https in the web address, https://waec.org.ng, is called a
questions.explanation: HTTPS is a protocol for secure communication.

questions.question: The process of transferring a file from a server to a computer is called
questions.explanation: Downloading transfers files from server to local machine.

questions.question: The following are functions of database management system except
questions.explanation: Formatting is usually done outside DBMS functions.

questions.question: Input devices are computer devices that can

options.A: send data and instructions into the computer memory.
options.B: be used to get data from the computer.
options.C: send results to the memory of the computer.
options.D: receive data and instructions from the computer memory.

questions.explanation: Input devices send data to the computer.

questions.question: Viruses, worms and Trojan horses are regarded as
questions.explanation: They are malicious software or malware.

questions.question: Which of the following actions is not an animation in Microsoft PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: Dancing is not a standard PowerPoint animation effect.

questions.question: The database model that uses tables with columns and rows is called
questions.explanation: Relational databases are based on tables with rows and columns.

questions.question: Datatypes in QBASIC programming language include the following except
questions.explanation: QBASIC does not have a 'float' datatype; it uses 'Single' and 'Double'.

questions.question: A program that converts source code instructions in high-level programming language to object codes line by line is known as
questions.explanation: Interpreter translates line by line at runtime.

questions.question: Stored instructions and data in digital computer consist of
questions.explanation: Data in computers is stored as bits.

questions.question: The process of making copies of files which can be used to restore the original copy in the event of data loss is called file
questions.explanation: Backing up files allows restoration after data loss.

questions.question: The primary purpose of an operating system is to
questions.explanation: OS manages hardware and software resources.

questions.question: Which device is used to output sound from a computer?
questions.explanation: Speakers output sound.

questions.question: In a relational database, data is stored in
questions.explanation: Relational databases use tables with rows and columns.

questions.question: The main function of the ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit) is to

options.C: control the input and output devices.

questions.explanation: ALU performs arithmetic and logic operations.
